mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in consumer price inflation indicate robust demand growth without inflationary pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns when RSAFS rises and CPIAUCSL falls, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days specifically during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during expansion phases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple quarters, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4% monthly, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces negative forward returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when tested against a baseline model without cross-terms.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts indicate rising residential investment and construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during periods of low interest rates.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or the signal reverses direction during high-rate environments.
